pr.title: Add Alfred to Development Tools
pr.body:
Adds Alfred, a self-hosted runtime that turns scoped GitHub issues into reviewed pull requests through autonomous Codex CLI and Claude Code agents.

### Where it goes
Section: Tools -> Development Tools (next to bernstein, SwarmClaw, claude-squad, vibekit, and other multi-agent orchestrators that drive Codex and Claude Code together).

### Why it fits
- Alfred drives Codex CLI as a first-class engine, alongside Claude Code, with role-based routing (e.g. implementer on Claude Code, reviewer on Codex).
- Self-hosted on macOS/Linux; no provider API keys, no cloud agent service.
- GitHub-native intake: scoped issues come in, reviewed pull requests come out. Per-firing git worktrees keep concurrent agents isolated.
- Label-driven state machine (`agent:implement` -> `agent:in-flight` -> `agent:pr-open` -> `agent:done`) and Slack reporting.

### Project link
https://github.com/luminik-io/alfred-os

### Docs
https://alfred.luminik.io/
pr.author: prasadus92
